title: calystegia silvatica subsp. disjuncta
intro: Calystegia silvatica subsp. disjuncta, also known as the Contra Costa morning glory, is a subspecies of the Calystegia silvatica plant, a flowering vine native to the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its high content of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Calystegia silvatica subsp. disjuncta include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms. Calystegia silvatica subsp. disjuncta can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
